data.current_description: Deletes a customer profile.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a customer profile document from the CL (Customer) entity in Master Data. Requires the document ID as a path parameter. Returns a 200 status code on successful deletion. This operation is irreversible and will remove all associated customer data. Requires appropriate License Manager permissions (Full access to all documents or Master Data administrator role).
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal. It lacks clarity about what entity is being modified (CL), what parameters are required, what the response indicates, and the irreversible nature of the deletion. Returns the document with updated attachment metadata. Files are persisted to the document's specified field.

data.current_description:
Creates a new document with an ID automatically generated by VTEX, corresponding to a given data entity. The request body is a JSON object where the key is the name of the field.

>ℹ️ You can use this request to create documents in any given data entity. Because of this, you are not restricted to using the fields exemplified below in your requests. But you should be aware of the fields allowed or required for each document you wish to update.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new document in the specified data entity with an auto-generated ID. The request body should be a JSON object with field names as keys and values matching the entity's defined field types. Returns the created document with its generated ID and metadata. The document is immediately persisted in Master Data.
data.reasoning: Original description mentions field types but doesn't clearly state what is returned or that the document is persisted. Enhanced description specifies the response includes the generated ID, clarifies the immediate persistence side effect, and improves AI agent understanding of when to use this operation versus other document creation methods.

data.current_description: Creates a partial document, sending only some of the fields.
data.summary_suggestion: Create or update document with partial fields
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new document or updates an existing one by sending only a subset of fields. The request body should contain only the fields to be set or modified. If no document exists, a new one is created with the provided fields. If a document exists, only the specified fields are updated while others remain unchanged. Returns the document with its ID and updated metadata.

data.current_summary: Create document with custom ID or update entire document
data.current_description:
Creates a new document with a custom ID, or updates an entire document if there is already a document with the informed ID.

>ℹ️ You can use this request to create or update documents in any given data entity. Because of this, you are not restricted to using the fields exemplified below in your requests. But you should be aware of the fields allowed or required for each document you wish to update.
data.summary_suggestion: Create or replace entire document by ID
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new document with a specified custom ID, or completely replaces an existing document if one already exists with that ID. The request body should contain all fields for the document; any fields not provided will be cleared if updating an existing document. Returns the created or updated document with its ID and metadata. This is an upsert operation that performs a full replacement, not a partial update.
data.reasoning: Summary is awkwardly phrased and doesn't clearly convey the upsert nature. Description lacks clarity about the full replacement behavior and what happens to unprovided fields. Enhanced summary uses clearer language, and description explicitly states this is a full replacement operation (not partial), clarifies field handling, and specifies the return value for better AI agent understanding of when to use PUT vs PATCH.

message: Operation 'Putversion' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original summary and description are misleading - 'update version' suggests modifying a version, but the operation actually restores/reverts to a version. This is critical for AI agent understanding. Missing details about what the operation actually does (restores state), required inputs, side effects (creates new version), and output. data.current_description: Updates the version of a document.
data.summary_suggestion: Restore document to a previous version
data.description_suggestion: Restores a document to a specific previous version state. Requires the data entity acronym, document ID, and version ID. This operation applies the state of the specified version to the current document, effectively reverting any changes made after that version. Returns the restored document object. Creates a new version entry in the document's version history. Requires Insert or update document permissions.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are misleading - 'update version' suggests modifying a version, but the operation actually restores/reverts to a version. This is critical for AI agent understanding. Missing details about what the operation actually does (restores state), required inputs, side effects (creates new version), and output. Clarified the action as 'restore' and added context about version history implications.

data.current_description: Retrieves Master Data v1 documents' information, while choosing which fields will be returned and filtering documents by specific fields.
data.summary_suggestion: Query and filter documents with field selection
data.description_suggestion: Searches and retrieves documents from a data entity with flexible filtering and field selection. Supports simple filters, complex queries with boolean operators, date ranges, numeric ranges, and partial text matching. Returns an array of document objects containing only the specified fields. Supports pagination through _offset and _limit parameters. Use this endpoint for queries under 10,000 documents; use Scroll endpoint for larger datasets. Avoid excessive wildcard searches to prevent rate limiting (429 errors).
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic and doesn't convey the operation's key capabilities (filtering, field selection). Description lacks output details (what is returned), pagination information, and guidance on when to use this versus Scroll. Added specificity about return format, pagination support, use case differentiation, and performance warnings to improve AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Retrieves a file that had been previously saved in a field of type `File`.

>⚠️ Make sure to include the file extension in the name, like in this example: `/api/dataentities/CL/documents/123/file/attachments/image.png`.
data.summary_suggestion: Download a file attachment from a document
data.description_suggestion: Downloads a file attachment that was previously saved in a File-type field of a document. Requires the data entity acronym, document ID, field name, and complete filename with extension. Returns the binary file content with appropriate content-type headers. The filename must include the file extension (e.g., image.png, document.pdf). Useful for retrieving customer-uploaded files, documents, or media associated with Master Data records.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic and doesn't clarify that this retrieves files/attachments. Description lacks context about required inputs (field name, filename with extension), what is returned (binary file content), and use cases. Added specificity about parameter requirements, return format, and practical examples to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.
